docs.name: Call of Cthulhu
docs.version: 7th Edition (percentile / open BRP mechanics) — uses open Basic Roleplaying d100 conventions and public-domain Lovecraft Mythos entities. Not affiliated with or endorsed by Chaosium Inc.
docs.author: XRPG Reference (open BRP d100)
docs.summary: A percentile horror system in which fragile, mortal investigators confront a cosmos indifferent to humanity. Roll 1d100 under a skill or characteristic to succeed; the deeper you peer into the Mythos, the faster your Sanity erodes. Combat is short, brutal, and usually best avoided.

dice.summary: Roll 1d100 equal to or under your skill/characteristic to succeed. ≤ half the value is a Hard success, ≤ one-fifth is an Extreme success. A roll of 01 is a Critical; 100 (or 96–100 when the skill is under 50) is a Fumble.

attributes.name: Strength
attributes.desc: Physical power; sets damage bonus and build alongside SIZ, and resists physical force.

attributes.name: Constitution
attributes.desc: Health and stamina; with SIZ it sets hit points and resists poison, disease, and exhaustion.

attributes.name: Size
attributes.desc: Mass and height; raises hit points and damage bonus but makes you easier to hit and harder to hide.

attributes.name: Dexterity
attributes.desc: Agility, speed, and fine coordination; sets combat order and governs Dodge and physical finesse.

attributes.name: Appearance
attributes.desc: Physical attractiveness and presence; influences first impressions and social interaction.

attributes.name: Intelligence
attributes.desc: Reasoning and memory; sets Idea rolls and the number of personal-interest skill points.

attributes.name: Power
attributes.desc: Force of will and spiritual strength; sets starting Sanity, Magic Points, and Luck recovery.

attributes.name: Education
attributes.desc: Formal learning and accumulated knowledge; sets occupation skill points and Know rolls.

attributeRules.note: Characteristics range roughly 15–90 for ordinary adults (default 50). Generate by rolling dice (e.g. 3D6×5 or 2D6+6 ×5) or by distributing points; the engine treats each as a percentile target. APP, SIZ, INT, and EDU often run higher than the physical traits. Max 99.

deriveds.name: Hit Points
deriveds.desc: Physical health. At 0 you fall Dying or Unconscious. Damage equal to half max HP in one blow is a Major Wound.

deriveds.name: Magic Points
deriveds.desc: Fuel for spells and magical resistance. Recovers fully after a night's rest; spent MP returns at about 1 per hour.

deriveds.name: Sanity
deriveds.desc: Mental stability, starting equal to POW. Lost to horror and the Mythos; hitting 0 means permanent, incurable madness. Max Sanity is reduced by Cthulhu Mythos skill.

deriveds.name: Luck
deriveds.desc: A spendable pool of fortune (roll 3D6×5 at the table). Spend points to push a failed roll up to success; it does not regenerate within a session.

deriveds.name: Movement
deriveds.desc: Base move rate 8 for typical adults. Higher when both STR and DEX exceed SIZ; lower when both are below it. Reduced by age over 40.

deriveds.name: Damage Bonus / Build
deriveds.desc: From STR+SIZ: 2–64 → −2 / −1 DB (Build −2/−1); 65–124 → none (Build 0); 125–164 → +1D4 (Build 1); 165–204 → +1D6 (Build 2). Add DB to melee and thrown damage.

easeLadder.name: Regular
easeLadder.use: Roll ≤ the full skill value. The default difficulty for most actions.

easeLadder.name: Hard
easeLadder.use: Roll ≤ half the skill value. The task is genuinely difficult or opposed by a capable foe.

easeLadder.name: Extreme
easeLadder.use: Roll ≤ one-fifth the skill value. A near-impossible feat or a master-level opponent.

easeLadder.name: Note
easeLadder.use: Call of Cthulhu sets difficulty by raising the success threshold, not by modifying the roll. The Keeper picks Regular / Hard / Extreme before you roll; Bonus and Penalty Dice handle situational swings (see reference).

conditions.name: Insane (Temporary)
conditions.effect: Triggered by losing 5+ Sanity at once or failing a Sanity roll after a single shock. The investigator is overwhelmed for 1D10 hours, suffering a bout of madness (panic, fugue, hysteria, catatonia) chosen or rolled by the Keeper.

conditions.name: Insane (Indefinite)
conditions.effect: Triggered by losing 20% or more of current Sanity within a single day. Lasts until significant in-game therapy and recovery; the investigator carries a lasting disorder (phobia, mania, delusion, amnesia) that resurfaces under stress.

conditions.name: Major Wound
conditions.effect: Caused by a single injury equal to or greater than half maximum HP. Requires a CON roll to stay conscious, drops the victim Prone, halves movement, and risks Dying if HP later reaches 0 without First Aid.

conditions.name: Dying
conditions.effect: At 0 HP with an unhealed Major Wound. Each round make a CON roll; failure loses 1 HP. A successful First Aid roll stabilizes the victim, otherwise death follows.

conditions.name: Unconscious
conditions.effect: At 0 HP without a Major Wound (or from certain shocks). The investigator is helpless and out of the scene until they recover at least 1 HP through First Aid or rest.

conditions.name: Phobia
conditions.effect: An irrational, overwhelming fear of a specific thing or situation, usually gained from indefinite insanity. Encountering the trigger forces the investigator to flee, freeze, or roll to act at all.

conditions.name: Mania
conditions.effect: An obsessive compulsion or fixation gained from indefinite insanity. The investigator is driven to indulge the mania, often to their own detriment, and resists being pulled away from it.

weapons.name: Unarmed (Brawl)
weapons.notes: Fists, kicks, grapples. Can subdue rather than kill. Fighting (Brawl) skill, base 25%.

weapons.name: Knife (small)
weapons.notes: Concealable melee weapon. Quiet and reliable.

weapons.name: Club / Blackjack
weapons.notes: Improvised or purpose-made bludgeon; can knock out.

weapons.name: Hatchet / Machete
weapons.notes: Heavy edged weapon; impaling on a critical or Extreme hit.

weapons.name: Shotgun (12-gauge)
weapons.notes: Damage by range band (close / medium / long). Devastating up close.

weapons.name: Dynamite (stick)
weapons.notes: Thrown explosive; full damage at center, halved nearby. Loud and indiscriminate.

bestiary.name: Cultist
bestiary.statblock:
**A devotee of some forbidden god, ready to kill or die for it.**

STR 50 · CON 55 · SIZ 55 · DEX 50 · POW 60 · INT 60

- **HP** 11 · **Build** 0 · **DB** none · **MOV** 8
- **Attacks** Knife 45% (1D4+DB) · Handgun 40% (1D10) · Fighting (Brawl) 45% (1D3+DB)
- **Skills** Occult 35%, Stealth 40%, Cthulhu Mythos 10–20% (some know a spell or two)
- **Sanity Loss to see** 0/1D4 if mid-ritual or visibly deranged; otherwise none — they look like ordinary people, which is the point.

bestiary.name: Hired Thug
bestiary.statblock:
**A mercenary, enforcer, or bootlegger paid to make problems disappear.**

STR 65 · CON 60 · SIZ 65 · DEX 55 · POW 45 · INT 50

- **HP** 12 · **Build** 1 · **DB** +1D4 · **MOV** 8
- **Attacks** Fighting (Brawl) 55% (1D3+1D4) · Club 50% (1D6+1D4) · Handgun 45% (1D10)
- **Skills** Intimidate 50%, Drive Auto 45%, Spot Hidden 40%
- **Sanity Loss to see** none — fully, dangerously human.

bestiary.name: Feral Dog
bestiary.statblock:
**A starving, half-wild dog; deadly in numbers.**

STR 35 · CON 50 · SIZ 25 · DEX 65 · POW 40

- **HP** 7 · **Build** −1 · **DB** −1 · **MOV** 10
- **Attacks** Bite 40% (1D6 − DB; on a successful bite it holds on and worries, automatic damage next round) · Pack tactics grant gangs of dogs bonus dice.
- **Skills** Track (by scent) 70%, Listen 60%, Spot Hidden 50%
- **Sanity Loss to see** none.

bestiary.name: Zombie (Reanimated Corpse)
bestiary.statblock:
**A shambling corpse raised by Mythos sorcery; slow, relentless, hard to stop.**

STR 70 · CON 60 · SIZ 60 · DEX 35 · POW 40

- **HP** 12 · **Build** 1 · **DB** +1D4 · **MOV** 6
- **Attacks** Fighting (Brawl) 35% (1D3+1D4, claw or grasp) · **Armor:** ignores impaling weapons' extra damage; takes only minimal harm from firearms — destroy the head or hack it apart.
- **Skills** none of note; mindlessly pursues its quarry.
- **Sanity Loss to see** 1/1D8.

bestiary.name: Ghoul
bestiary.statblock:
**A loping, dog-faced eater of the dead that haunts tunnels, crypts, and graveyards.**

STR 65 · CON 65 · SIZ 65 · DEX 65 · POW 65 · INT 65

- **HP** 13 · **Build** 1 · **DB** +1D4 · **MOV** 9 (faster in tunnels)
- **Attacks** 3 attacks/round — 2 Claws & 1 Bite, Fighting 35% (Claw 1D6+DB; Bite 1D6 + worry). A successful bite holds and tears for automatic damage.
- **Skills** Climb 85%, Jump 75%, Listen 70%, Scent Prey 75%, Stealth 65%
- **Sanity Loss to see** 0/1D6.

bestiary.name: Deep One
bestiary.statblock:
**A fish-frog hybrid from the drowned cities of the sea; effectively immortal, growing larger with age.**

STR 75 · CON 65 · SIZ 65 · DEX 55 · POW 65 · INT 65

- **HP** 13 · **Build** 1 · **DB** +1D4 · **MOV** 8 / 10 swimming
- **Attacks** 3 attacks/round — Claws & Bite, Fighting 45% (Claw 1D6+DB). Some elders wield tridents or know spells.
- **Skills** Swim 90%, Listen 50%, Spot Hidden 50%; many serve Dagon, Hydra, or Cthulhu.
- **Armor** 1 point of scaly hide.
- **Sanity Loss to see** 0/1D6.

bestiary.name: Elder Thing
bestiary.statblock:
**A barrel-bodied, starfish-headed, winged being — the 'Old Ones' who built cities before mankind.**

STR 75 · CON 80 · SIZ 85 · DEX 45 · POW 65 · INT 90

- **HP** 16 · **Build** 2 · **DB** +1D6 · **MOV** 8 / 10 swim / 12 fly
- **Attacks** up to 5 tentacle attacks/round, Fighting 40% (Tentacle 1D6 + crush). Highly intelligent; uses tools, surgery, and Mythos science.
- **Skills** Cthulhu Mythos 35%+, immense scientific knowledge; revives from frozen dormancy.
- **Sanity Loss to see** 0/1D6.

bestiary.name: Nightgaunt
bestiary.statblock:
**A black, rubbery, faceless winged thing with barbed tail and horns; servitor of Nodens and the Outer Gods.**

STR 65 · CON 65 · SIZ 65 · DEX 75 · POW 65 · INT 50

- **HP** 13 · **Build** 1 · **DB** +1D4 · **MOV** 8 / 12 flying
- **Attacks** Grab & carry off — Fighting 40%; on a grapple it seizes a victim, flies aloft, and tickles toward unconsciousness or drops them.
- **Special** silent and faceless; immune to ordinary weapons in some tellings (Keeper's discretion).
- **Sanity Loss to see** 0/1D6.

bestiary.name: Dimensional Shambler
bestiary.statblock:
**A loping, clawed thing that steps between dimensions to seize its prey and drag it away.**

STR 100 · CON 75 · SIZ 80 · DEX 55 · POW 65 · INT 50

- **HP** 15 · **Build** 2 · **DB** +1D6 · **MOV** 7
- **Attacks** 2 Claws/round, Fighting 45% (Claw 1D8+DB). On grabbing a victim with both claws it can shamble out of reality, taking them with it.
- **Special** can walk between dimensions at will; hard to corner or trap.
- **Sanity Loss to see** 0/1D10.

bestiary.name: Star Vampire
bestiary.statblock:
**An invisible, titanically strong horror that giggles as it drains its victims; only visible when gorged with blood.**

STR 105 · CON 60 · SIZ 95 · DEX 50 · POW 50 · INT 50

- **HP** 16 · **Build** 3 · **DB** +1D6 · **MOV** 8 / 13 flying
- **Attacks** Grab & drain — Fighting 50% (Tentacle/grasp 1D6+DB, then drains blood for automatic damage each round it holds).
- **Special** invisible until it feeds, when blood reveals its monstrous outline. A horrible bell-like tittering announces it.
- **Sanity Loss to see** 1/1D10 (more when its bloated form becomes visible).

bestiary.name: Hunting Horror
bestiary.statblock:
**A vast, ropy, serpentine flyer of living darkness summoned by Nyarlathotep; destroyed by daylight.**

STR 110 · CON 90 · SIZ 100 · DEX 80 · POW 90 · INT 60

- **HP** 19 · **Build** 3 · **DB** +1D6 · **MOV** 8 / 16 flying
- **Attacks** Bite & coil, Fighting 70% (Bite 1D8+DB; Coil grapples and crushes). Can swallow a grappled victim whole.
- **Special** vulnerable to sunlight — direct daylight banishes or destroys it. A creature of pure roiling night.
- **Sanity Loss to see** 1D3/1D20.

bestiary.name: Shoggoth
bestiary.statblock:
**A mountainous, shapeless mass of black, bubbling protoplasm that throws up temporary eyes, mouths, and limbs. 'Tekeli-li!'**

STR 100+ · CON 200+ · SIZ 200+ · DEX 35 · POW 60 · INT 50

- **HP** 40+ · **Build** 7+ · **DB** +5D6 · **MOV** 10
- **Attacks** Crush & engulf, Fighting 30% (Crush 5D6+; engulfs and dissolves victims). Can split off lesser masses.
- **Special** nearly impossible to kill with conventional weapons; regenerates; fire and overwhelming force only. Stats vary wildly by mass.
- **Sanity Loss to see** 1D6/1D20.

bestiary.name: Byakhee
bestiary.statblock:
**A composite winged horror — part insect, part vampire bat, part decayed humanoid — that flies the void between stars.**

STR 65 · CON 55 · SIZ 65 · DEX 65 · POW 65 · INT 50

- **HP** 12 · **Build** 1 · **DB** +1D4 · **MOV** 5 / 20 flying
- **Attacks** Bite & claw, Fighting 40% (Claw 1D6+DB; Bite drains 1D6 blood on a hold). Can be summoned and ridden across space with the proper spell and the Space-Mead.
- **Sanity Loss to see** 1/1D6.

bestiary.name: Mi-go (Fungi from Yuggoth)
bestiary.statblock:
**A pinkish, fungoid, crustacean-like flying alien that mines Earth and carries human brains in canisters between worlds.**

STR 65 · CON 65 · SIZ 55 · DEX 65 · POW 65 · INT 75

- **HP** 12 · **Build** 1 · **DB** +1D4 · **MOV** 7 / 9 flying (even in the void)
- **Attacks** 2 Claws/round, Fighting 50% (Nip 1D6+DB; a successful grab pins a limb). Often armed with alien tools and weapons.
- **Special** can fly through space; surgically extracts brains; their wings work poorly in Earth's atmosphere yet still bear them aloft.
- **Sanity Loss to see** 0/1D6.

bestiary.name: Fire Vampire
bestiary.statblock:
**A darting spark of malevolent living fire, spawn of Cthugha; it feeds on vitality and ignites whatever it touches.**

STR — · CON 10 · SIZ 1 · DEX 90 · POW 60 · INT 30

- **HP** 1 (but tiny and incredibly fast) · **MOV** 11 flying
- **Attacks** Touch 80% — a flaring contact deals 1D6 fire and drains MP; sets flammable things alight. Swarms arrive in numbers.
- **Special** extinguished by water or smothering, but a single spark can grow into a conflagration.
- **Sanity Loss to see** 0/1D6.

bestiary.name: Rat Swarm
bestiary.statblock:
**A boiling carpet of rats — sewer-dwellers, sometimes more than rats in the deep places.**

Treat as a single SIZ-spanning hazard rather than individuals.

- **HP** (the swarm) 6 · **MOV** 7
- **Attacks** Bites cover anyone in the swarm's space, dealing 1 point per round, ignoring most armor; spreads disease.
- **Special** flows around obstacles, into vents and drains. Fire and flooding disperse it.
- **Sanity Loss to see** none (0/1 if it is clearly not ordinary vermin).

tables.name: Bout of Madness (1D10)
entries: Amnesia — the investigator blanks the horror, coming to with no memory of recent minutes.
Psychosomatic disability — sudden blindness, deafness, or paralysis of a limb.
Violence — lashes out at the nearest target, friend or foe.
Babbling — collapses into incoherent muttering, weeping, or laughter.
Flight — runs in blind panic, heedless of danger, until exhausted.
Physical hysterics — uncontrollable laughing, crying, or screaming.
Echopraxia — mindlessly mimics those around them.
Faints dead away, helpless until roused.
Fugue — wanders off in a trance, later 'waking' somewhere else with no memory.
A new phobia or mania takes root then and there.

tables.name: Sample Phobias (1D10)
entries: Nyctophobia — fear of the dark.
Claustrophobia — fear of enclosed spaces.
Hemophobia — fear of blood.
Thalassophobia — fear of the sea and deep water.
Necrophobia — fear of the dead and corpses.
Acrophobia — fear of heights.
Entomophobia — fear of insects and crawling things.
Autophobia — fear of being alone.
Taphophobia — fear of being buried alive.
Spectrophobia — fear of mirrors and one's own reflection.

tables.name: Sample Manias (1D10)
entries: Kleptomania — compulsion to steal.
Pyromania — compulsion to set fires.
Megalomania — obsession with one's own power and destiny.
Bibliomania — obsession with collecting books, especially forbidden ones.
Trichotillomania — compulsive pulling of one's own hair.
Graphomania — compulsive, obsessive writing.
Dipsomania — uncontrollable craving for alcohol.
Religious mania — fanatical, all-consuming devotion.
Nymphomania / erotomania — fixation on a person or desire.
Hoarding — compulsion to accumulate and never discard.

tables.name: Forbidden Tomes (1D6)
entries: A water-stained occult treatise in crabbed Latin marginalia.
A privately printed account of a vanished expedition.
A coded diary whose final pages devolve into raving.
A fragmentary translation of something far older.
An illustrated grimoire whose plates should not be looked at long.
A pamphlet from a defunct cult, disturbingly persuasive.

reference.title: The Percentile Roll
reference.body:
When an action is uncertain and failure matters, roll **1D100** (two ten-sided dice, one as tens and one as units; 00 + 0 reads as **100**). You **succeed if the roll is equal to or less than** your skill or characteristic value.

- **01–05** Always a success (a roll of 01 is a **Critical**).
- **Roll ≤ value** → **Regular success.**
- **Roll > value** → failure.
- **96–100** (or **100** for skills of 50+) → **Fumble** — something goes badly wrong.

Most actions need no roll: only call for one when the outcome is in doubt **and** failure is interesting. The Keeper decides which skill applies and how hard the task is before any dice hit the table.

reference.title: Hard & Extreme Success
reference.body:
Call of Cthulhu raises difficulty by **lowering the threshold you must roll under**, not by penalizing the roll:

| Difficulty | Succeed if you roll ≤ |
|---|---|
| **Regular** | the full skill value |
| **Hard** | one-half the value (round down) |
| **Extreme** | one-fifth the value (round down) |

The Keeper sets the level **before** you roll, based on the situation or the quality of the opposition. Degrees of success also matter in opposed contests and for special effects (e.g. an Extreme combat success may impale or maximize damage). A **Critical (01)** is the best possible result.

reference.title: Pushing Rolls
reference.body:
Fail a roll and there is usually no second chance — **unless you push it.** To push, describe how you redouble your effort, take more time, or accept greater risk, then **reroll the same skill once**.

- A pushed **success** resolves the action.
- A pushed **failure** lets the Keeper introduce a serious, fiction-driven consequence — far worse than the original failure (the rope snaps, the cultists hear you, the page crumbles to dust).

You cannot push every roll: combat rolls, Sanity rolls, and Luck rolls **cannot** be pushed. Pushing is the engine of dramatic escalation — offer it when the investigators are stuck, and make the stakes real.

reference.title: Bonus & Penalty Dice
reference.body:
Situational advantage and disadvantage are handled with extra **tens dice** rather than flat modifiers.

- A **Bonus Die**: roll an extra tens die and **keep the better (lower) tens result**.
- A **Penalty Die**: roll an extra tens die and **keep the worse (higher) tens result**.
- Multiple bonus and penalty dice **cancel one for one**; apply the net remainder.

Good lighting, the right tool, or an ally's help might grant a bonus die; darkness, a moving target, or a wound might impose a penalty die. The units die is unchanged — only which tens you keep changes.

reference.title: Luck
reference.body:
**Luck** is a spendable pool (roll **3D6×5** at creation). It represents sheer fortune and fate's thin mercy.

- **Spend Luck** to raise a failed **skill or characteristic roll** up to a success, point for point — but never on a Sanity roll, a Luck roll itself, or to change a Critical/Fumble.
- The Keeper may call for a **Luck roll** (roll ≤ current Luck) to resolve pure chance, like whether a stray bullet finds you or a needed item happens to be at hand.
- Spent Luck does **not** return during a session; long-term recovery comes between scenarios. Spend it wisely — it is often the difference between an investigator who lives and one who doesn't.

reference.title: Skills & Characteristics
reference.body:
Investigators act through **skills** (Spot Hidden, Library Use, Psychology, Stealth, Drive Auto, Persuade, Dodge, First Aid, Fighting, Firearms, Occult, and many more), each rated 0–99%. Each has a **base chance** before training (e.g. Spot Hidden 25%, Listen 20%, Library Use 20%, Psychology 10%, Persuade 10%, Dodge = half DEX).

- **Characteristic rolls** test raw STR/CON/SIZ/DEX/APP/INT/POW/EDU as a percentile (and **Idea** = INT, **Know** = EDU, **Luck** rolls).
- During play, **succeeding** on a skill marks it for **improvement**; between scenarios, roll above the skill on 1D100 to gain **+1D10%** in it.
- **Cthulhu Mythos** is the dread exception: every point gained **reduces maximum Sanity** by an equal amount.

reference.title: Combat: Order & Actions
reference.body:
Combat runs in rounds of a few seconds. Each round:

1. **Order by DEX** — highest DEX acts first (those readying firearms may go on their DEX before melee; ties resolve by higher DEX or the Keeper's call).
2. On your turn take **one action** (attack, move-and-act, or maneuver) plus a short move.
3. Resolve attacks with the relevant **Fighting** or **Firearms** skill.

Damage from a successful hit is the weapon's dice plus, for melee/thrown, the **Damage Bonus (DB)**. An **Extreme** success or a **Critical** with an impaling weapon maximizes or doubles damage. Combat is deadly fast — investigators who treat every fight as winnable do not last.

reference.title: Fighting Back & Dodging
reference.body:
When attacked in melee, the target chooses how to respond — this is an **opposed roll**:

- **Dodge** (skill = half DEX) — try to avoid the blow entirely. You may Dodge any number of times in a round, but doing so is your action.
- **Fight Back** — meet the attack with your own Fighting skill; whoever rolls the **better level of success** lands their blow (the defender can hurt the attacker).
- **Both roll**; the higher success level wins, with ties going to the defender. A **Fumble** while defending is its own catastrophe.

Firearms generally can't be dodged at range (you can only dive for cover); they're resisted by Luck, cover, and not standing in the open.

reference.title: Damage, Major Wounds & Healing
reference.body:
Subtract damage from **Hit Points** (HP = (CON + SIZ) ÷ 10).

- A **single injury ≥ half your maximum HP** is a **Major Wound**: make a CON roll or fall **Unconscious**; you are knocked **Prone** and your movement is halved.
- At **0 HP with no Major Wound** → **Unconscious**. At **0 HP with a Major Wound** → **Dying** (lose 1 HP per round on a failed CON roll until stabilized).
- **First Aid** (one attempt per wound) restores **1 HP** and stabilizes the Dying. **Medicine** does more over time.
- Natural healing returns about **1 HP per day** (more with successful Medicine). A Major Wound heals only after a successful weekly Medicine or First Aid check.
- At **0 HP that drops below your limit, or a killing blow**, the investigator dies.

reference.title: Sanity & SAN Rolls
reference.body:
**Sanity (SAN)** starts equal to POW and measures how much cosmic horror a mind can bear.

- When you witness something horrifying, the Keeper calls a **Sanity roll**: roll **1D100 ≤ current SAN**.
 - **Success** → lose the smaller listed amount (often 0 or 1).
 - **Failure** → lose the larger amount (e.g. **0/1D6**, **1/1D8**, **1D6/1D20** for the worst horrors).
- You lose Sanity only **once** per specific source the first time you confront it.
- **Maximum Sanity** equals 99 minus your **Cthulhu Mythos** skill — knowledge of the Mythos permanently caps how sane you can ever be.
- Sanity recovers slowly: surviving and resolving a scenario, successful therapy, and confronting (and overcoming) the source of a disorder.

reference.title: Going Insane
reference.body:
Sanity loss can tip an investigator over the edge:

- **Losing 5+ Sanity from a single source at once** forces an **Intelligence (Idea) roll**; on a success the full horror sinks in and the investigator suffers a **bout of madness** — **Temporary Insanity** (1D10 hours or a scene).
- **Losing 20% or more of current Sanity within a single day** triggers **Indefinite Insanity** — a lasting disorder (phobia, mania, delusion, amnesia) lasting until real recovery.
- **Sanity 0** → **permanent, incurable insanity**; the investigator is lost to the Mythos and leaves play.

During a bout of madness, roll or choose a result (see the *Bout of Madness* table). Underscores of horror — phobias and manias — resurface whenever the trigger reappears. Reaching the edge and stepping back is the heart of the game.

reference.title: Chases & Pursuit
reference.body:
When someone flees and someone follows, run a **chase** rather than rolling once:

1. Establish each participant's effective **MOV** (modified by Build, terrain, vehicles, injuries, and age). Faster parties gain extra **chase actions** each round.
2. Lay out the route as a series of **locations**, some with **hazards** (a fence, traffic, a crowd) that demand a skill roll (Climb, Drive Auto, Dodge, etc.).
3. Each round, movers spend actions to clear hazards and close or open the gap; failing a hazard roll costs distance or inflicts a setback.
4. The chase ends when a pursuer reaches the quarry (combat or capture) or the quarry breaks line of sight and escapes.

Chases keep flight tense and tactical without reducing escape to a single die.

reference.title: Investigation & Clues
reference.body:
The core of the game is **finding things out.** Useful tools:

- **Spot Hidden** to notice details; **Library Use** to research records and tomes; **Listen** to overhear; **Psychology** to read a person's intent; **Persuade / Fast Talk / Intimidate / Charm** to get cooperation.
- **Idea** (INT) rolls let players connect clues they've gathered; **Know** (EDU) rolls recall facts a character would plausibly know.
- A guiding principle: **don't gate essential clues behind a single roll.** Failure should cost time or add danger, not stall the scenario. Let rolls reveal *extra* detail or *easier* paths instead.

Note any **forbidden tomes** found: reading one grants Cthulhu Mythos and perhaps a spell, at the price of Sanity and a permanently lowered Sanity ceiling.

reference.title: Magic, Spells & Magic Points
reference.body:
Mythos **magic** is rare, costly, and dangerous — and it warps the caster.

- Spells are **learned**, usually from tomes or instruction, never assumed.
- Casting spends **Magic Points (MP = POW ÷ 5)** and often **Sanity**; some spells also demand POW, blood, or time.
- **MP** recovers with rest (roughly one per hour, all after a full night's sleep). MP can also fuel magical **resistance** in opposed contests of will (POW vs POW).
- Investigators are not wizards: a spell is a desperate, often horrifying option, not a reliable resource. Casting visibly inhuman magic can itself cost Sanity, both for the caster and for any onlookers.

reference.title: Creating an Investigator
reference.body:
Build an ordinary person who will face the extraordinary:

1. **Characteristics** — generate STR, CON, SIZ, DEX, APP, INT, POW, EDU (each on a percentile scale; e.g. roll 3D6×5, or 2D6+6 ×5 for SIZ, INT, EDU). Default near 50.
2. **Derived stats** — **HP** = (CON+SIZ)÷10; **MP** = POW÷5; **Sanity** = POW; **Luck** = 3D6×5; **MOV** by comparing STR/DEX to SIZ (and age); **Damage Bonus/Build** from STR+SIZ.
3. **Occupation** — choose one; it sets occupation skill points (a multiple of EDU and one other characteristic) and a list of professional skills.
4. **Personal interests** — INT×2 points spread across any skills.
5. **Backstory** — beliefs, significant people, meaningful locations, treasured possessions, and traits. These anchor the human cost of the horror to come.

reference.title: Running Horror (Keeper's Notes)
reference.body:
- **Dread over gore.** The fear is anticipation, helplessness, and wrongness — not viscera.
- **Combat is a failure state, not the goal.** Most Mythos creatures outclass investigators; the smart play is to flee, outwit, or avoid.
- **Pace the Sanity loss.** Save the big SAN hits for true revelations; let smaller horrors accumulate.
- **Reward investigation.** Don't bottleneck the plot on one roll; let clues flow and let failure raise the cost, not stop the story.
- **Offer pushed rolls** when players are stuck, and make the consequences bite.
- **The cosmos doesn't care.** Victories are partial and temporary; survival with sanity intact is its own triumph.

rollPresets.name: Spot Hidden
rollPresets.note: Skill: Spot Hidden (base 25%). Notice concealed details, objects, or dangers.

rollPresets.name: Listen
rollPresets.note: Skill: Listen (base 20%). Overhear or detect sound.

rollPresets.name: Library Use
rollPresets.note: Skill: Library Use (base 20%). Research records, archives, and tomes.

rollPresets.name: Psychology
rollPresets.note: Skill: Psychology (base 10%). Read a person's intent or sincerity.

rollPresets.name: Persuade
rollPresets.note: Skill: Persuade (base 10%). Win someone over with reasoned argument.

rollPresets.name: Dodge
rollPresets.note: Skill: Dodge (base = half DEX). Avoid an incoming attack as your action.

rollPresets.name: Fighting (Brawl)
rollPresets.note: Skill: Fighting (Brawl) (base 25%). Unarmed/melee attack; damage 1D3 + DB.

rollPresets.name: Firearms (Handgun)
rollPresets.note: Skill: Firearms (Handgun) (base 20%). Ranged attack; can't usually be Dodged.

rollPresets.name: Stealth
rollPresets.note: Skill: Stealth (base 20%). Move unseen and unheard.

rollPresets.name: First Aid
rollPresets.note: Skill: First Aid (base 30%). Restore 1 HP and stabilize the Dying.

rollPresets.name: Sanity Roll
rollPresets.note: Roll ≤ current SAN. Failure loses the larger listed amount. Cannot be pushed.

rollPresets.name: Luck Roll
rollPresets.note: Roll ≤ current Luck for pure chance. Or spend Luck to upgrade a failed skill roll.

rollPresets.name: Idea Roll (INT)
rollPresets.note: Connect clues, recall the obvious, or grasp a situation.

rollPresets.name: Know Roll (EDU)
rollPresets.note: Recall a fact the investigator would plausibly know.

docs.npcStatFormat: STR / CON / SIZ / DEX / APP / INT / POW / EDU | HP | Build/DB | SAN loss
